modules.title: Linux Fundamentals
modules.focus: Master Linux from absolute beginner to intermediate level through hands-on projects
modules.output: Ability to navigate, read, search, script, and analyze systems on Linux — the hacker's primary tool

topics.title: Project 1: Hello Terminal — Your First Commands
topics.description: Learn what a terminal is, why hackers use it, and your first 5 commands: whoami, pwd, ls, echo, clear.

topics.title: Project 2: Files & Directories — Building Your Lab
topics.description: Navigate the filesystem, create directories, move and delete files. Build your pentest workspace.

topics.title: Project 3: Reading Files — Extracting Intelligence
topics.description: Read files using cat, head, tail, wc, and file. Learn why reading files is the #1 hacker skill.

topics.title: Project 4: Searching — Finding Needles in Haystacks
topics.description: Search inside files with grep, find files by name/size/type with find, and locate executables with which.

topics.title: Project 5: Permissions — Who Can Do What
topics.description: Understand Linux permissions (rwx), ownership (user/group), and why SUID binaries are the #1 privilege escalation vector.

topics.title: Project 6: Processes — What's Running
topics.description: View running processes with ps and top, kill processes, and understand process states. Learn to identify suspicious processes.

topics.title: Project 7: Pipes & Redirects — Chaining Commands
topics.description: Chain commands together with pipes, redirect output to files, and build powerful one-liners for recon.

topics.title: Project 8: Bash Scripting — Writing Your First Tools
topics.description: Write bash scripts with variables, conditionals, and loops. Automate reconnaissance and build custom tools.

topics.title: Project 9: Log Analysis — Reading the Battlefield
topics.description: Analyze auth.log, syslog, and application logs to detect attacks, find patterns, and extract indicators of compromise.

topics.title: Project 10: System Recon — Know Your Target
topics.description: Perform full system enumeration: OS version, users, network, services, scheduled tasks. The first step of any pentest.

modules.title: Linux Projects
modules.focus: Build real security tools using everything you learned in the Linux tutorials
modules.output: 4 portfolio-ready scripts: recon tool, intrusion detector, privesc checker, network analyzer

topics.title: Project: Automated Recon Tool
topics.description: Build a bash script that automatically enumerates a target system — ports, services, SUID binaries, users, cron jobs. The first tool every hacker writes.

topics.title: Project: Real-Time Intrusion Detector
topics.description: Build a script that monitors auth.log in real-time, detects brute force attacks, and alerts when thresholds are exceeded. The foundation of every SOC tool.

topics.title: Project: Privilege Escalation Checker
topics.description: Build a script that checks a system for privilege escalation vectors — SUID binaries, sudo misconfigs, writable files, cron jobs. What every pentester runs first.

topics.title: Project: Network Forensics Analyzer
topics.description: Build a script that analyzes network traffic logs, extracts IPs, counts connections, and detects port scan patterns. The analyst's eyes on the network.

modules.title: What a Computer Is
modules.focus: Set up your lab environment and understand how computers work at a fundamental level
modules.output: Fully configured WSL2 Ubuntu environment with all security tools installed, and understand the input-process-output cycle

topics.title: Your Toolkit Setup (Do This First)
topics.description: Install WSL2, Ubuntu, and all security tools before starting Day 1. This is your lab environment.

topics.title: The Input-Processing-Storage-Output Cycle
topics.description: Every computer follows one fundamental pattern: data comes in, gets processed, is stored somewhere, and results come out.

topics.title: Hardware Components
topics.description: Inside every computer is a collection of physical components that work together. Learn what each component does, how they connect, and what to look for when evaluating hardware.

topics.title: Operating Systems - Windows, Linux, macOS
topics.description: An operating system manages hardware resources and provides an interface for users and applications. Compare the three major desktop operating systems.

modules.title: Daily Computer Use
modules.focus: Master essential everyday computer skills and file management
modules.output: Navigate operating systems efficiently, manage files and folders, and handle basic software and browser tasks

topics.title: Keyboard, Mouse, and Shortcuts
topics.description: Develop efficient input skills that save hours of time. Learn keyboard shortcuts for Windows and Linux that professionals use daily.

topics.title: File Management - Folders, Copy, Paste, Search
topics.description: Effective file organization saves time and prevents data loss. Learn to create, move, rename, and search for files using GUI and command line methods.

topics.title: Software Management, Browsers, and Troubleshooting
topics.description: Install, update, and remove software safely. Navigate the web effectively and perform basic troubleshooting.

modules.title: Internet & Online Safety
modules.focus: Understand how the internet works and stay safe online
modules.output: Explain networking basics, practice email security, and implement strong authentication habits

topics.title: How the Internet Works - URLs, HTTP, DNS
topics.description: Every time you visit a website, a complex chain of events happens in milliseconds. Understand URLs, protocols, and DNS.

topics.title: Email Basics and Phishing Awareness
topics.description: Email is the primary attack vector for most cyberattacks. Learn how email works, identify phishing attempts, and protect yourself.

topics.title: Passwords, MFA, and Safe Browsing
topics.description: Your password is the first line of defense. Learn strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, and safe browsing habits.

modules.title: Operating System Basics
modules.focus: Navigate and manage Windows and Linux operating systems
modules.output: Use Windows system tools, understand Linux basics, and manage system resources

topics.title: Windows Desktop, Settings, and File Explorer
topics.description: Master the Windows interface - navigate settings, customize the desktop, and use File Explorer efficiently.

topics.title: Windows Task Manager, Device Manager, and Utilities
topics.description: Use Windows built-in tools to monitor performance, manage hardware, and diagnose system issues.

topics.title: Linux Introduction - Terminal, Commands, Directory Structure
topics.description: Get comfortable with the Linux terminal. Learn essential commands, understand the filesystem hierarchy, and navigate with confidence.

modules.title: First Command Line Skills
modules.focus: Build practical command line proficiency through hands-on exercises
modules.output: Navigate filesystems, manipulate files, write simple scripts, and combine commands from the terminal

topics.title: Navigation Commands - cd, ls, pwd, mkdir, touch
topics.description: Master filesystem navigation in the terminal. Create directories, generate files, and build muscle memory with path manipulation.

topics.title: File Operations - cp, mv, rm, cat, less, head, tail
topics.description: Practice copying, moving, deleting, and viewing files from the command line with real-world exercises.

topics.title: Simple Scripts and Combining Commands
topics.description: Write basic shell scripts, chain commands together, and build practical automation for daily tasks.

modules.title: Environment Setup
modules.focus: Installing WSL2, Ubuntu, security tools, and organizing your lab workspace
modules.output: Fully configured WSL2 Ubuntu environment with all security tools installed and a GitHub repo for write-ups

topics.title: WSL2 + Ubuntu Installation
topics.description: Enable WSL2 on Windows, install Ubuntu, configure Windows Terminal, and understand the Windows/Linux filesystem bridge.

topics.title: Security Tool Installation
topics.description: Install and verify essential security tools: VS Code with WSL extension, Burp Suite, Wireshark, nmap, gobuster, nikto, sqlmap, John the Ripper, Hashcat, and utility packages.

topics.title: Lab Organization & GitHub
topics.description: Create a structured lab workspace, initialize a GitHub repository for write-ups, and verify all tools are operational.

topics.title: Linux File System & Permissions
topics.description: Navigate the Linux directory structure, understand file types, and master permissions with chmod, chown, chgrp, SUID, SGID, and the sticky bit.

topics.title: Linux Process Management & Services
topics.description: View, manage, and control running processes using ps, top, kill, jobs, crontab, and systemd systemctl commands.

modules.title: Python for Security Automation
modules.focus: Python fundamentals, security libraries, and building custom scanning tools
modules.output: Working Python scripts for port scanning, directory brute-forcing, log parsing, and security header analysis

topics.title: Python Foundations
topics.description: Master Python basics: variables, data types, strings, loops, conditionals, functions, and file I/O with security-focused examples.

topics.title: Security Libraries & Scripts
topics.description: Use Python's requests, socket, hashlib, and re modules to build a log parser and HTTP security header checker.

topics.title: Port Scanner & Directory Brute-Forcer
topics.description: Build a threaded TCP port scanner and a directory brute-forcing tool from scratch using Python's socket and requests modules.

topics.title: Text Processing for Security
topics.description: Master grep, sed, and awk for log analysis, parsing /etc/passwd, extracting failed SSH attempts, and building security analysis pipelines.

topics.title: Network Fundamentals with Linux Tools
topics.description: Use ip, ping, traceroute, dig, nslookup, curl, wget, ss, and iptables to understand and manage Linux networking.

modules.title: OSI Model & Packet Theory
modules.focus: Master the OSI model, TCP/UDP protocols, and port-based service enumeration
modules.output: Ability to map network traffic to OSI layers and identify services via port scanning

topics.title: OSI Model Deep Dive
topics.description: Learn all 7 layers of the OSI model, the protocols at each layer, encapsulation/decapsulation, PDU names, and security relevance.

topics.title: TCP/UDP & The Three-Way Handshake
topics.description: Master TCP and UDP protocols, the three-way handshake, connection teardown, and packet analysis with Wireshark.

topics.title: Ports, Protocols & Banner Grabbing
topics.description: Learn common port/protocol mappings, use Nmap and Netcat for banner grabbing, and understand why service version detection matters.

modules.title: IP Addressing & Subnetting
modules.focus: Master binary math, IP addressing, CIDR notation, subnetting, DHCP, and ARP
modules.output: Ability to perform subnet calculations, understand network segmentation, and analyze DHCP/ARP traffic

topics.title: Binary Math & IP Structure
topics.description: Convert between binary and decimal, understand IP address structure, IP classes, and subnet masks.

topics.title: CIDR, Subnetting & Private Ranges
topics.description: Perform CIDR subnetting, understand private IP ranges, NAT, and subnet calculation exercises.

topics.title: DHCP, ARP & Subnet Drills
topics.description: Understand the DHCP DORA process, ARP resolution, ARP spoofing attacks, and complete subnetting drills.

modules.title: DNS, DHCP & HTTP Deep Dive
modules.focus: Master DNS resolution, HTTP methods and headers, TLS handshake, and traffic analysis
modules.output: Ability to analyze DNS queries, inspect HTTP traffic, and understand TLS encryption

topics.title: DNS Resolution Chain
topics.description: Understand the complete DNS resolution process, record types, dig/nslookup commands, and DNS security risks.

topics.title: HTTP Methods, Headers & TLS
topics.description: Master HTTP methods, request/response structure, important headers, and the TLS handshake process.

topics.title: HTTP Security & Wireshark Analysis
topics.description: Inspect HTTP traffic in Wireshark, identify cleartext credentials, analyze security headers, and complete a hands-on exercise.

modules.title: Network Security Architecture
modules.focus: Understand firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPNs, tunneling, and implement defense in depth with a UFW capstone lab
modules.output: Ability to configure network security controls and implement defense in depth strategies

topics.title: Firewalls & IDS/IPS
topics.description: Learn about packet filtering, stateful, and application-layer firewalls, plus the differences between IDS and IPS.

topics.title: VPNs, Tunneling & Defense in Depth
topics.description: Learn about VPN types, WireGuard setup, SSH tunneling, and the defense in depth security model.

topics.title: UFW Capstone Lab
topics.description: Install and configure UFW on Ubuntu, create firewall rules, test with nmap, and verify Phase 2 knowledge.

modules.title: Threats, Attacks & Vulnerabilities Part 1
modules.focus: Core security concepts, threat actors, and malware fundamentals
modules.output: Ability to classify threats, identify malware types, and explain CIA triad failures

topics.title: CIA Triad, AAA & Threat Actors
topics.description: Deep dive into the foundational security model, authentication/authorization/accounting, and the landscape of threat actors and their motivations.

topics.title: Malware Types & Attack Vectors
topics.description: Comprehensive overview of malware categories, their behaviors, delivery mechanisms, and real-world examples.

modules.focus: Social engineering techniques and cryptography fundamentals
modules.output: Ability to identify social engineering attacks, explain cryptographic concepts, and understand PKI basics

topics.title: Social Engineering
topics.description: Understanding psychological manipulation techniques, phishing email analysis, and organizational defense strategies.

topics.title: Encryption & Hashing Fundamentals
topics.description: Core cryptographic concepts including symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing algorithms, digital signatures, and PKI infrastructure.

modules.title: Architecture & Implementation Part 1
modules.focus: Identity and access management fundamentals and access control models
modules.output: Ability to implement IAM solutions, configure MFA, and apply appropriate access control models

topics.description: Comprehensive coverage of authentication factors, MFA technologies, SSO/federation protocols, and directory services.

topics.title: Access Control Models
topics.description: Detailed comparison of DAC, MAC, RBAC, and ABAC models with implementation guidance and security principles.

modules.title: Architecture & Implementation Part 2
modules.focus: Network security architecture, virtualization technologies, and system resilience
modules.output: Ability to design secure networks, implement zero trust, and configure high availability

topics.title: Secure Network Design
topics.description: Network security architecture including DMZ, segmentation, zero trust principles, and cloud security models.

topics.title: Virtualization & Resilience
topics.description: Hypervisor types, containerization, high availability, load balancing, backup strategies, and RAID configurations.

modules.title: Operations & Incident Response Part 1
modules.focus: Incident response lifecycle and logging/monitoring fundamentals
modules.output: Ability to execute IR procedures, understand NIST IR lifecycle, and configure logging infrastructure

topics.title: Incident Response Lifecycle
topics.description: NIST incident response lifecycle, CSIRT composition, evidence handling, and playbooks for common scenarios.

topics.title: Logging & Monitoring
topics.description: Windows and Linux log management, syslog fundamentals, SIEM concepts, and log retention policies.

modules.title: Operations & Incident Response Part 2
modules.focus: Vulnerability management, digital forensics, and data destruction
modules.output: Ability to perform vulnerability scans, forensic analysis, and proper data sanitization

topics.title: Vulnerability Management
topics.description: Vulnerability scanning vs penetration testing, CVE/CVSS scoring, and patch management lifecycle.

topics.title: Digital Forensics & Data Destruction
topics.description: Forensic imaging, memory forensics, chain of custody, and proper data sanitization methods.

modules.title: Governance, Risk & Compliance Part 1
modules.focus: Risk management frameworks and NIST standards
modules.output: Ability to perform risk assessments, apply NIST frameworks, and classify security controls

topics.description: Risk identification, assessment, response strategies, quantitative vs qualitative analysis, and risk register creation.

topics.description: NIST RMF (SP 800-37), NIST CSF, NIST SP 800-53 controls, and security control categories.

modules.title: Governance, Risk & Compliance Part 2
modules.focus: GDPR overview and NDPR deep dive for data protection compliance
modules.output: Ability to explain GDPR/NDPR requirements and implement data protection controls

topics.title: GDPR Overview
topics.description: General Data Protection Regulation fundamentals including data processing principles, data subject rights, and breach notification.

topics.title: NDPR Deep Dive
topics.description: Nigeria Data Protection Regulation requirements, NDPC oversight, data subject rights, and comparison with GDPR.

modules.title: Cryptography & PKI Part 1
modules.focus: Symmetric/asymmetric cryptography and hashing for password security
modules.output: Ability to apply cryptographic algorithms, understand key management, and implement secure password storage

topics.title: Symmetric & Asymmetric Crypto
topics.description: AES key sizes and modes, RSA key generation, ECC efficiency, Diffie-Hellman key exchange, and forward secrecy.

topics.title: Hashing & Password Security
topics.description: SHA-256, SHA-512, HMAC, bcrypt, argon2, rainbow tables, and practical hashing operations.

modules.title: Cryptography & PKI Part 2
modules.focus: Digital signatures, certificates, and comprehensive Phase 3 review
modules.output: Ability to manage certificate lifecycles, understand revocation mechanisms, and demonstrate Security+ domain mastery

topics.title: Digital Signatures & Certificates
topics.description: How digital signatures work, certificate lifecycle, CRL vs OCSP, and Let's Encrypt implementation.

topics.title: Phase 3 Review & Practice
topics.description: Comprehensive review of all Security+ domains covered in Phase 3 with practice scenarios and assessment.

modules.title: PortSwigger Setup & SQL Injection Basics
modules.focus: Environment setup, Burp Suite proxy configuration, and fundamental SQL injection techniques
modules.output: Burp Suite configured, first SQLi labs completed, understanding of injection mechanics

topics.title: PortSwigger & Burp Suite Setup

topics.title: SQL Injection: Authentication Bypass
topics.description: Understand how SQL injection breaks authentication, master login bypass payloads, and explore error-based SQLi fundamentals.

topics.title: SQL Injection: UNION-Based Extraction
topics.description: Master UNION-based data extraction with ORDER BY column counting, type detection, and systematic data exfiltration techniques.

modules.title: Advanced SQLi & XSS Foundations
modules.focus: Advanced SQL injection techniques and introduction to cross-site scripting
modules.output: Understanding of second-order and out-of-band SQLi, ability to exploit reflected XSS

topics.title: SQL Injection: Advanced Techniques
topics.description: Explore second-order SQL injection, out-of-band data exfiltration via DNS/HTTP, and learn prevention techniques including parameterized queries.

topics.title: Cross-Site Scripting: Reflected XSS
topics.description: Learn XSS fundamentals, exploit reflected XSS in URL parameters, and understand HTML context payloads.
topics.labTitle: Reflected XSS: HTML Context Nothing Encoded

modules.title: XSS Advanced: Stored & DOM-Based
modules.focus: Persistent XSS via database storage and client-side DOM manipulation attacks
modules.output: Ability to exploit stored and DOM-based XSS vulnerabilities

topics.title: Cross-Site Scripting: Stored XSS
topics.description: Exploit persistent XSS through comment fields, profile pages, and database storage that affects every visitor.
topics.labTitle: Stored XSS: HTML Context Single Variable

topics.title: Cross-Site Scripting: DOM XSS
topics.description: Learn how DOM-based XSS works through source-to-sink analysis, and exploit vulnerabilities in client-side JavaScript.

modules.title: XSS Bypass & Authentication Security
modules.focus: Advanced XSS filter bypass, session management, and authentication attack techniques
modules.output: Ability to bypass XSS filters and exploit authentication mechanisms

topics.title: XSS Filter Bypass & Exploitation
topics.description: Bypass common XSS filters using event handlers, restricted tags/attributes, and understand CSP bypass techniques.
topics.labTitle: Reflected XSS: Most Tags and Attributes Blocked

topics.title: Session Management & Authentication
topics.description: Understand session tokens, cookie attributes, session fixation attacks, and brute force protection mechanisms.

topics.title: Authentication Attacks
topics.description: Exploit password reset flows through host header injection, perform 2FA bypass techniques.

modules.title: JWT Security
modules.focus: JWT structure, signature verification attacks, and algorithm confusion exploitation
modules.output: Ability to exploit JWT weaknesses including unverified signatures and algorithm confusion

topics.title: JWT Structure & Attacks
topics.description: Understand JWT header/payload/signature structure, exploit unverified signatures, and brute force weak signing keys.
topics.labTitle: JWT Authentication Bypass via Unverified Signature

topics.title: JWT Algorithm Confusion
topics.description: Exploit RS256 to HS256 algorithm confusion attacks, understand how public key signing compromises asymmetric JWT implementations.
topics.labTitle: JWT Authentication Bypass via Algorithm Confusion

modules.title: API Security
modules.focus: REST API reconnaissance and exploitation, GraphQL introspection and attack techniques
modules.output: Ability to test and exploit REST and GraphQL APIs

topics.title: REST API Security
topics.description: Discover API endpoints through documentation, exploit BOLA/IDOR vulnerabilities, and test for mass assignment flaws.
topics.labTitle: API Testing: Exploiting an API Endpoint Using Documentation

topics.title: GraphQL Security

modules.title: SSRF & Request Smuggling

topics.title: Request Smuggling
